core_mechanism: measuring a predictor's genuine skill as its performance in excess of a trivial base-rate null model, validated by paired significance tests on out-of-sample (expanding walk-forward, held-out) partitions with false-discovery-rate control across multiple comparisons
brief_reason: The method's whole logic is that raw accuracy is confounded by the marginal base rate of the majority outcome, so skill is defined as excess accuracy over an always-majority null and confirmed via paired tests (McNemar, Diebold-Mariano) under Benjamini-Hochberg FDR control on walk-forward folds.
